key_insight: A hybrid GNN-LSTM surrogate can learn how short-fiber thermoplastic microstructure drives static mechanical response, enabling fast prediction of stiffness and stress-strain behavior from reconstructed cells.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical route to screen additively manufactured and compression-molded SFT parts for weak regions and support digital-twin workflows without running full mesoscale FE on every design.
method: Reconstruct microstructures from micro-CT data and partition them into Voronoi-based interaction cells.
Homogenize each cell with nonlinear finite element simulations that include matrix damage.
Train a GNN-LSTM to encode microstructural topology and history-dependent response.
evidence_signal: The surrogate matches unseen microstructures with about R^2 ≈ 0.98 versus high-fidelity FE while reducing computation by more than two orders of magnitude.
watch_item: Performance depends on calibrated damage laws and representative training microstructures, so extrapolation to new manufacturing conditions or geometries may be limited.
paper_title: On Surrogate Modeling of Static Response of AM Short-Fiber Thermoplastics Using Graph Neural Networks
